question: The difference between revenue and capital expenditure
options: Central banks creating money to buy assets and stimulate the economy
Every expense to be justified from scratch each period
Revenue is day-to-day spending; capital is on long-term assets
Taking greater risks because the consequences are borne by others
answer: Revenue is day-to-day spending; capital is on long-term assets

question: A rights issue differs from a bonus issue because
options: As price level falls, real purchasing power rises
A period of rising share prices and investor confidence
A period of falling share prices and low investor confidence
Rights issues raise new capital; bonus issues convert reserves to shares
answer: Rights issues raise new capital; bonus issues convert reserves to shares

question: The aggregate demand curve slopes downward because
options: Setting prices very low to drive competitors out of the market
As price level falls, real purchasing power rises
Every expense to be justified from scratch each period
Central banks creating money to buy assets and stimulate the economy
answer: As price level falls, real purchasing power rises

question: Deadweight loss occurs in a monopoly because
options: Business performance across financial, customer, internal and learning perspectives
Overhead costs to products based on the activities that cause them
Owns enough shares in other companies to control them
Output is restricted below the socially optimal level
answer: Output is restricted below the socially optimal level

options: Rights issues raise new capital; bonus issues convert reserves to shares
Setting prices very low to drive competitors out of the market
